results.possibleIdentity: Ronald F. Cross was an electrical engineer active in Canada during the 1960s and 1970s. He graduated with a degree in Electrical Engineering from the University of British Columbia (UBC) in 1962, where he was recorded as a resident of West Vancouver. Following his graduation, he moved to Toronto, Ontario, where he became an engineer for the Square D Company, a major manufacturer of electrical equipment and power distribution systems. His technical expertise focused on electrical and power engineering, specifically alternating current (AC) machines and motor controls. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, he was associated with the Toronto chapter of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that promoted collaborations between the technical community and artists. His participation in E.A.T. occurred during a peak period of the organization's international expansion, which included active local groups in major Canadian cities like Toronto and Vancouver.
